title: Duomo
content:
Duomo (,) is an Italian term for a church with the features of, or having been built to serve as, a cathedral, whether or not it currently plays this role. Monza Cathedral, for example, has never been a diocesan seat and is by definition not a cathedral.

On the other hand, the city of Trevi no longer has a bishop, although it once did, and the erstwhile cathedral of Emilianus of Trevi is now a mere church. By contradistinction, the Italian word for a cathedral "sensu stricto" is "cattedrale". Many people refer to particular churches simply as "il Duomo", the "Duomo", without regard to the full proper name of the church.
